title: Dead Code, Unused Dependencies, and Reachability · Spiral Review
summary: Remove unreachable code and dependencies with evidence rather than guesswork. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a dead-code and dependency audit after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat unused imports, variables, parameters, private members, and exports according to language semantics.
Detect unreachable branches, impossible states, and obsolete feature paths.
Detect unused, missing, duplicate, and undeclared dependencies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — Use a changed review case for gates dead code that tests this competency: Remove dead code with tests, telemetry, deprecation, and rollback evidence proportionate to risk.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Safe Removal trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for remove dead code with tests, telemetry, deprecation, and rollback evidence proportionate to risk.

content.text: For Safe Removal, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Safe Removal precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case for gates dead code that tests this competency: Remove dead code with tests, telemetry, deprecation, and rollback evidence proportionate to risk.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Deleting unused-looking code is always a harmless cleanup.

stimulus.title: Safe Removal case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case for gates dead code that tests this competency: Remove dead code with tests, telemetry, deprecation, and rollback evidence proportionate to risk.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Deleting unused-looking code is always a harmless cleanup.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Remove dead code with tests, telemetry, deprecation, and rollback evidence proportionate to risk. Artifact under review: a dead-code and dependency audit.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, apply Safe Removal without its stated preconditions, omit the a dead-code and dependency audit trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Safe Removal for a dead-code and dependency audit.
The record names the assumption, applies Safe Removal, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Safe Removal record says a dead-code and dependency audit looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve separate evidence from confidence.

steps.title: Record what required effort

Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Safe Removal through the concrete a dead-code and dependency audit case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Remove dead code with tests, telemetry, deprecation, and rollback evidence proportionate to risk.
